name: Almond Lace Rolls
recipeInstructions:
1. Finely grind the almonds.
2. Preheat oven to 350°F and butter a baking sheet with flour.
3. In a small, heavy-bottomed pan, combine all ingredients.
4. Cook over low heat, stirring constantly until the butter melts.
5. Drop by teaspoonful onto the prepared baking sheet (do not bake more than six at one time).
6. Bake for 7-9 minutes, or until only slightly brown and still bubbling at the center.
7. Allow cookies to cool for a minute or so until the edge is firm enough to lift with a thin spatula.
8. Working quickly, lift the cookies and turn them top side down onto paper towels.
9. Roll over the handle of a wooden spoon to achieve a cylinder shape and allow to cool until crisp.
10. Use a fresh baking sheet for each batch to prevent cookies from sticking.
11. If cookies become too firm to roll over the spoon handle, place them back in the oven for a few moments.
12. Store cookies in an airtight cookie jar or tin; they will keep for a long time and can be made well ahead of the holiday season.
13. Variations: The cookies can also be left flat, making them smaller if desired; however, they won't have the rolled lacy effect. Alternatively, roll ends of cooled cookies in melted chocolate.
Yield: 2 dozen
